title: helianthemum hirtum subsp. hirtum
intro: Helianthemum hirtum subsp. hirtum, also known as the hairy rockrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The hairy rockrose has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
